current_desc: Customer ID - platform-internal primary key. Assigned at registration. Unique within etoro DB. Used as the universal customer identifier across all tables
source_desc: Customer ID - platform-internal primary key. Assigned at registration. Unique within etoro DB. Used as the universal customer identifier across all tables
judge_reason: The downstream description is identical to the source description.

current_desc: Account registration date. Default=getdate() at production. Indexed via Idx_Customer_Customer_Registered with INCLUDE on key contact fields in production. In this DWH table, serves as the base for DateID and TimeID derivation
source_desc: Account registration date. Default=getdate(). Indexed via Idx_Customer_Customer_Registered with INCLUDE on key contact fields
judge_reason: Both describe the account registration date with the same default and index; the downstream note about DateID/TimeID derivation is additive DWH context, not a change in business meaning.

current_desc: Whether this is a real-money account (1) or demo (0). In production this is a bit column; in DWH stored as tinyint. All non-demo customers have IsReal=1
source_desc: Whether this is a real-money account (1) or demo (0). All rows in this environment are IsReal=1. Demo accounts may be in separate DB or schema
judge_reason: Both describe a real-money (1) vs demo (0) flag with the same business meaning; the added detail about bit vs tinyint storage is a harmless implementation note.

current_desc: Platform identifier. In production Customer.CustomerStatic this is a nullable int with 4 known values: 0=Undefined, 1=Web, 2=IOS, 3=Android (confirmed from Dim_Platform lookup)
source_desc: Platform/product identifier (web, mobile, etc.)
judge_reason: Both describe a platform/product identifier; the downstream version simply adds implementation details (nullable int, known enum values) that do not change the business meaning.

current_desc: Primary key identifying the account state. 1=Open (active account, full platform access), 2=Closed (deactivated, no activity permitted), 0=N/A (DWH placeholder for NULL-safe JOINs). Referenced by Dim_Customer.AccountStatusID
source_desc: Primary key identifying the account state. 1=Open (active account, full platform access), 2=Closed (deactivated, no activity permitted). Referenced by Customer.CustomerStatic.AccountStatusID and Hedge.AccountStatus tables. See [Account Status](_glossary.md#account-status). (Dictionary.AccountStatus)
judge_reason: The downstream description adds a DWH-specific placeholder value (0=N/A) for NULL-safe JOINs, which is a standard warehouse implementation detail that does not change the business meaning of the source column.

current_desc: Human-readable label for the account state: 'Open', 'Closed', or 'N/A'. Used in reporting to display account state. Sourced directly from Dictionary.AccountStatus.AccountStatusName
source_desc: Human-readable label for the account state. Used in BackOffice reporting procedures (e.g., BackOffice.GetBlockedCustomers, BackOffice.GetClosedAccountsByLastChangeDate) to display account state in administrative UI
judge_reason: Both describe AccountStatusName as a human-readable label for account state used in reporting; the downstream version adds example enum values but does not change the business meaning.

current_desc: Primary key identifying the account classification. 0=N/A (DWH placeholder), 1=Private, 2=Corporate, 3=IB Account, 4=Joint Account, 5=White Label, 6=Affiliate Private, 7=Employee, 8=Custodian, 9=Fund, 10=eToro Group, 11=News, 12=White List, 13=Analyst, 14=SMSF, 15=Affiliate Corporate, 16=Administrated, 17=Funded Employee, 18=Trust. Controls feature access, regulatory treatment, fee structures, and compliance monitoring level. Referenced by Dim_Customer.AccountTypeID
source_desc: Primary key identifying the account classification. 1=Private, 2=Corporate, 3=IB Account, 4=Joint, 5=White Label, 6=Affiliate Private, 7=Employee, 8=Custodian, 9=Fund, 10=eToro Group, 11=News, 12=White List, 13=Analyst, 14=SMSF, 15=Affiliate Corporate, 16=Administrated, 17=Funded Employee. Controls feature access, regulatory treatment, fee structures, and compliance monitoring level. See [Account Type](_glossary.md#account-type). (Dictionary.AccountType)
judge_reason: Downstream adds DWH placeholder 0=N/A and 18=Trust which are legitimate DWH/downstream extensions of the same enumeration; core business meaning and all shared enum values match.

current_desc: Human-readable account type label. Maps to Dictionary.AccountType.AccountTypeName in production (renamed in DWH). Used in reporting to display account classification
source_desc: Display name for the account type. UPPERCASE format matching Apex Clearing's API conventions
judge_reason: Both describe the display name for the account type; the downstream description adds lineage detail but refers to the same column value.

current_desc: Primary key. Manually assigned (no IDENTITY). Stable identifier for this payment gateway endpoint. Range 1-174 with gaps; 163 rows. Referenced by fact deposit/cashout tables and MID settings
source_desc: Primary key. Manually assigned (no IDENTITY). Stable identifier referenced by deposits, MID settings, and routing tables
judge_reason: Both describe DepotID as a manually-assigned primary key and stable identifier referenced by downstream billing/deposit tables; the downstream version simply adds cardinality details and synonyms.

current_desc: Payment method type (e.g., 1=CreditCard, 2=WireTransfer, 6=Neteller, 8=MoneyBookers). References Dictionary.FundingType. 38 distinct values across 163 depots
source_desc: Payment method type (e.g., 1=CreditCard, 2=Wire, 6=Neteller, 8=MoneyBookers/Skrill). References `Dictionary.FundingType` implicitly (no FK constraint in DDL). 38 distinct values across 163 depots
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same business meaning: a payment method type ID referencing Dictionary.FundingType with identical cardinality; minor wording differences (Wire vs WireTransfer, MoneyBookers/Skrill vs MoneyBookers) are cosmetic label variants.

current_desc: Direction of payment flow. 1=Deposit, 2=Cashout, 3=Refund. References Dictionary.PaymentType
source_desc: Deposit/Cashout/Refund direction
judge_reason: Both describe the payment direction (Deposit/Cashout/Refund); the downstream version simply adds the enum values and FK reference, which is additive detail, not a meaning change.

current_desc: Payment processing protocol/gateway. References Dictionary.Protocol. Identifies the specific API or connection (e.g., Protocol 7=Neteller, Protocol 6=Wire, Protocol 8=MoneyBookers)
source_desc: Payment processing protocol
judge_reason: The downstream description adds helpful examples and a dictionary reference but the core meaning — payment processing protocol — matches the source exactly.

current_desc: Human-readable depot name (e.g., 'MoneyBookers USD', 'Neteller', 'Wire'). Unique across all depots. Used in admin dashboards, routing logs, and discrepancy reports
source_desc: Human-readable depot name (e.g., 'MoneyBookers USD', 'Neteller', 'Wire'). UNIQUE (BDPT_NAME index). Used in admin dashboards, routing logs, and discrepancy reports
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same meaning: a unique human-readable depot name used in admin dashboards, routing logs, and discrepancy reports.

current_desc: Whether this depot currently accepts transactions. 1=Active (eligible for routing); 0 or NULL=Inactive (excluded from routing). 114 of 163 rows are active
source_desc: Whether this depot is currently accepting transactions. 1=Active (eligible for routing); 0 or NULL=Inactive (excluded from routing). 114 of 163 rows are active. Queried as `IsActive = 1` in routing logic
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same business meaning; the downstream version merely omits the implementation detail about how the column is queried.

current_desc: Surrogate primary key. Renamed from `ID` in the production Billing.ProtocolMIDSettings table. Referenced by fact deposit and withdrawal tables to record which routing configuration was used per transaction
judge_reason: source wiki found but no matching column row

current_desc: Protocol parameter type. Part of logical routing key. References Billing.Parameter which defines the parameter name/type (e.g., MID, SecretKey, ApiKey). Together with DepotID, DepotModeID, RegulationID, CurrencyID forms the unique routing key
source_desc: [CODE-BACKED] Protocol parameter type. Part of logical PK. References Billing.Parameter which defines the parameter name/type (e.g., "MID", "SecretKey", etc.)
judge_reason: Both describe ParameterID as a protocol parameter type referencing Billing.Parameter and part of the logical primary/routing key; the downstream version simply expands which columns form the composite key.

current_desc: Payment gateway/depot. Part of logical routing key. References Billing.Depot (DWH: Dim_BillingDepot.DepotID). Identifies the payment processor this MID configuration belongs to
source_desc: [CODE-BACKED] Payment gateway/depot. Part of logical PK. Identifies the payment processor this MID belongs to
judge_reason: Both describe DepotID as the payment gateway/depot identifying the payment processor; 'logical PK' vs 'logical routing key' and added DWH reference are minor wording/enrichment differences that preserve the same business meaning.

current_desc: Trading mode. Part of logical routing key. 0=General (applies to both), 1=Live, 2=Demo. Separates Live and Demo payment processing environments. ~60% Demo, ~37% Live
source_desc: [CODE-BACKED] Trading mode. Part of logical PK. 0=General, 1=Live, 2=Demo. Separates Live and Demo processing environments. 60% Demo, 37% Live
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same business meaning: a trading mode identifier (0=General, 1=Live, 2=Demo) that separates processing environments, with matching value distributions.

current_desc: The protocol identifier string (MID, merchant ID, API key, etc.) passed to the payment processor for routing. SENSITIVE -- contains payment gateway credentials. Examples: merchant ID numbers, API endpoint identifiers
source_desc: [CODE-BACKED] The protocol identifier string (MID, merchant ID, API key, etc.). This is the actual routing value passed to the payment processor. Examples: "18989693", "18986763"
judge_reason: The downstream description adds an unsupported claim that the field contains 'payment gateway credentials' and 'API endpoint identifiers', whereas the source describes it as a routing identifier (MID/merchant ID/API key string), not a credential or endpoint.
proposed_fix: The protocol identifier string (MID, merchant ID, API key, etc.). This is the actual routing value passed to the payment processor. Examples: "18989693", "18986763" (Tier 1 - Billing.ProtocolMIDSettings.md)

current_desc: Regulatory entity. Part of logical routing key. Segments MIDs by legal jurisdiction: 0=None, 1=CySEC (EU), 2=FCA (UK), plus additional ASIC/other values. Ensures transactions route through the correct legal entity's acquiring relationship
source_desc: [CODE-BACKED] Regulatory entity. Part of logical PK. Segments MIDs by legal jurisdiction (CySEC=1, FCA=2, etc.). Ensures transactions route through the correct legal entity's acquiring relationship
judge_reason: Both describe the same regulatory jurisdiction segmentation key for MID routing; the downstream version simply enumerates additional known enum values (0=None, ASIC) which is additive detail, not a meaning change.

current_desc: Currency restriction. Part of logical routing key. 0=any currency (most rows). Non-zero values restrict this MID entry to a specific transaction currency
source_desc: [CODE-BACKED] Currency restriction. Part of logical PK. 0=any currency. Most rows are 0. Non-zero restricts this MID to a specific currency
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same meaning: CurrencyID is a currency restriction field, part of the logical key, where 0 means any currency and non-zero restricts to a specific currency.

current_desc: Sub-routing type. 0=default routing (~94% of rows); 3=alternate sub-routing for specific processor subsets (~6% of rows). Allows multiple routing paths within the same (depot, mode, regulation, currency)
source_desc: [CODE-BACKED] Sub-routing type. 0=default (93.5%), 3=alternate (6.5%). Allows multiple routing paths within the same (depot, mode, regulation, currency)
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same business meaning: a sub-routing type with values 0 (default, ~94%) and 3 (alternate, ~6%) enabling multiple routing paths within the same key combination.

current_desc: Optional link to a specific merchant account configuration in Billing.MerchantAccountValues. When set (~25% of rows), enables finer-grained routing to a specific acquiring account within a depot. NULL when not applicable
source_desc: [CODE-BACKED] Optional link to a specific merchant account configuration. 377 rows (25.6%) have this set for finer-grained routing
judge_reason: Both describe an optional link to a merchant account configuration for finer-grained routing, with consistent ~25% population; the downstream version adds the target table name which is additive detail, not a meaning change.

current_desc: Primary key identifying the bonus category. 0=N/A (DWH placeholder), 1=First Registration Bonus, 2=Sales First Deposit Bonus, 3=Custom, ... 71=Credit Line. 66 rows, IDs 0-71 with gaps 60-65. Smallint in DWH vs int IDENTITY in production
source_desc: Auto-generated unique identifier for each bonus type. PK referenced by BackOffice.Bonus (BonusTypeID FK) and BackOffice.CampaignToBonusType. Also used as ParentID for child types in the hierarchy
judge_reason: Both describe BonusTypeID as the primary key identifying bonus types; the downstream description adds DWH-specific enumeration detail and datatype difference but the business meaning is the same.

current_desc: Internal BackOffice name for the bonus type. Used by BackOffice staff for reporting and operational routing. This is NOT the customer-facing label -- production also has a DisplayName column (excluded from DWH). Note: some names contain typos from production (e.g., "Cashout Fee Reimbursment" - misspelling of "Reimbursement")
source_desc: Internal name used by BackOffice staff for identification, reporting, and operational routing. Shown in the BackOffice UI dropdowns. NOT the customer-visible name - see DisplayName. Examples: "Dormant Fee", "Hedge Abuser", "Request for Documents", "Cashout Fee Reimbursment" (note: typo in production data). Has BBNT_NAME index
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same meaning: an internal BackOffice name (not customer-facing) used for reporting and routing, with a note about production typos.

current_desc: Whether the bonus amount can be withdrawn by the customer. False (0) for ALL 66 rows in the DWH -- this field is either a planned feature not yet activated or withdrawability is controlled elsewhere in the bonus lifecycle
source_desc: Whether the bonus amount can be withdrawn by the customer. Currently 0 (false) for ALL 70 active bonus types - this field is either a planned feature or bonus withdrawability is controlled elsewhere in the bonus lifecycle
judge_reason: Both describe the same boolean flag for bonus withdrawability, noting it is effectively unused; the row-count difference (70 vs 66) is a snapshot detail, not a meaning difference.

current_desc: Whether this bonus type is still in active use. False for IDs 0 (N/A placeholder), 17 (Refill-Negative Balance), and 23 (Championship Winner Demo). True for all other 63 types
source_desc: Whether this bonus type is still in active use. 0=deprecated (should not be assigned to new bonuses). Active=0 types: 17=Refill-Negative Balance, 23=Championship Winner Demo. All other 68 types are IsActive=1
judge_reason: Downstream adds ID 0 as inactive and counts 63 active types instead of 68, contradicting the source which only lists IDs 17 and 23 as inactive with 68 active types.
proposed_fix: Whether this bonus type is still in active use. 0=deprecated (should not be assigned to new bonuses). Active=0 types: 17=Refill-Negative Balance, 23=Championship Winner Demo. All other 68 types are IsActive=1 (Tier 1 - BackOffice.BonusType)

current_desc: Primary key. Auto-incrementing campaign identifier in production. PK NOT ENFORCED in DWH. Currently only ID=0 (N/A placeholder) exists
source_desc: Auto-incrementing campaign identifier. Clustered PK. NOT FOR REPLICATION. Referenced by BackOffice.CampaignToBonusType, Customer.Customer.CampaignID, and History.Credit
judge_reason: Both describe CampaignID as an auto-incrementing primary key campaign identifier; the downstream note about DWH enforcement and current data population is additive operational detail, not a meaning change.

current_desc: Campaign group for organization/reporting. FK to BackOffice.CampaignGroup in production. NULL for 30.5% of production campaigns (ungrouped)
source_desc: Campaign grouping for organization/reporting
judge_reason: Both describe a campaign grouping identifier for organization/reporting; the downstream version adds FK detail and NULL-rate context but the business meaning is identical.

current_desc: Unique public-facing campaign code (e.g., "20coupon", "freecopyref"). The identifier customers enter at registration. UNIQUE in production. Currently only 'N/A' (ID=0 placeholder)
source_desc: Unique public-facing campaign code. UNIQUE index. The identifier customers enter at registration (e.g., "20coupon", "freecopyref"). Auto-generated if not supplied to CampaignAdd: 6-char random string. CampaignBunchAdd generates longer codes with optional prefix. Case-sensitivity depends on server collation
judge_reason: The downstream description adds a claim that the column currently contains only 'N/A' (ID=0 placeholder), which is an unverified data-quality assertion not present in the source and could mislead analysts into thinking the column is unpopulated.
proposed_fix: Unique public-facing campaign code (e.g., "20coupon", "freecopyref"). The identifier customers enter at registration. UNIQUE index. Auto-generated 6-char random string if not supplied. (Tier 1 - BackOffice.Campaign)

current_desc: Maximum number of customers who can use this campaign. Range in production: 0 to 100,000,000. 0 in placeholder row
source_desc: Maximum number of customers who can use this campaign. When ParticipatedUsers reaches this cap, the campaign is full. Range: 0 (unlimited/test) to 100,000,000
judge_reason: Both describe the same cap on campaign participants with the same range; the downstream simplifies '0 (unlimited/test)' to '0 in placeholder row' but the business meaning is equivalent.

current_desc: Campaign activation datetime (UTC). '1900-01-01' in placeholder row
source_desc: Campaign activation datetime (UTC per CampaignEditActiveTime). Campaigns with StartDate < NOW at creation time are immediately activated (IsActive=1). SQL Agent job StartJobID automates activation for future-dated campaigns
judge_reason: Both describe the campaign activation datetime in UTC; the downstream note about a placeholder row is an additive implementation detail that does not change the column's business meaning.

current_desc: Campaign expiry datetime (UTC). Must be after StartDate. IsActive is NOT auto-set to 0 when EndDate passes in production. '1900-01-01' in placeholder row
source_desc: Campaign expiry datetime (UTC). Must be at least 60 minutes after StartDate and at least 60 minutes from now at creation/edit time. SQL Agent job EndJobID automates deactivation. Note: IsActive is NOT automatically set to 0 when EndDate passes - many "active" campaigns have past EndDates
judge_reason: Both describe the campaign expiry datetime in UTC with the same key caveat that IsActive is not auto-set to 0 when EndDate passes; the downstream omits the 60-minute minimum gap detail but this is an implementation constraint, not a different business meaning.

current_desc: Maximum total bonus pool in dollars. Range in production: $0 to $15,000,000. 0 in placeholder row
source_desc: Maximum total bonus pool for this campaign in dollars (stored as money type). **IMPORTANT**: CampaignAdd/CampaignBunchAdd/CampaignEdit receive the value in cents (integer) and store as CAST(@val AS MONEY)/100. So caller passes 1500000000 to store $15,000,000.00
judge_reason: Both describe the maximum total bonus pool in dollars; the downstream adds observed range and placeholder detail but the business meaning is identical.

current_desc: Whether campaign is active. 1=active, 0=inactive. False in placeholder row
source_desc: Whether the campaign is currently active and accepting new users. 1=active, 0=inactive. Auto-set at creation based on whether StartDate < NOW. Updated by CampaignEditActiveTime when dates change. NOT automatically updated when EndDate passes
judge_reason: Both describe a boolean flag indicating whether the campaign is active (1=active, 0=inactive); the downstream addition of placeholder-row behavior and omission of update mechanics do not change the business meaning.

current_desc: Count of customers who used this campaign. MASKED WITH default() - non-privileged users see 0. 0 in placeholder row
source_desc: Count of customers who have used this campaign. Initialized to 0 at creation. Incremented when a customer registers with this campaign Code. When ParticipatedUsers >= MaxNumberOfUsers, the campaign is full
judge_reason: Both describe the count of customers who used the campaign; the downstream version adds masking/placeholder details which are implementation-level annotations, not a change in business meaning.

current_desc: Human-readable campaign description. MASKED WITH default() - non-privileged users see NULL. NULL in placeholder row
source_desc: Human-readable description of the campaign's purpose or terms. 10,984 of 11,080 campaigns have descriptions
judge_reason: Both describe the same human-readable campaign description column; the downstream adds masking/access-control detail and a placeholder-row note, which are implementation details that do not change the business meaning.

current_desc: Card network identifier. Active brands: 1=Visa, 2=Master Card, 3=Diners, 8=Maestro. Inactive: 0=None, 4=Amex, 5=Fire Pay, 6=JCB, 7=American Express, 9=Laser, 10=Switch, 11=UK Local, 12=Discover, 13=Local Card, 14=China Union Pay, 15=Solo, 16=Cirrus, 17=GE Capital
source_desc: Card network identifier. Active brands: 1=Visa, 2=MasterCard, 3=Diners, 8=Maestro. Inactive: 0=None, 4=Amex, 5=FirePay, 6=JCB, 7=American Express, 9=Laser, 10=Switch, 11=UK Local, 12=Discover, 13=Local Card, 14=China UnionPay, 15=Solo, 16=Cirrus, 17=GE Capital, 18=Unknown, 19-31=various regional/legacy brands
judge_reason: Descriptions convey the same business meaning; the downstream version merely omits trailing inactive brands 18-31 and adds spaces in brand names, which does not change column interpretation for analysts.

current_desc: Card brand name. Unique constraint prevents duplicates in production. Used in payment UI, transaction records, and fraud reporting. Renamed from `Name` in production. 0=None, 1=Visa, 2=Master Card, 3=Diners, 4=Amex, 5=Fire Pay, 6=JCB, 7=American Express, 8=Maestro, 9=Laser, 10=Switch, 11=UK Local Credit Card, 12=Discover, 13=Local Card, 14=China Union Pay, 15=Solo, 16=Cirrus, 17=GE Capital
judge_reason: source wiki found but no matching column row

current_desc: Whether this card brand is currently accepted for deposits: 1=accepted, 0=rejected. Type widened from bit to int in DWH. Only 4 of 32 are currently active in production. DWH note: DWH snapshot values may differ from current production state
source_desc: Whether this card brand is currently accepted for deposits: 1=accepted, 0=rejected. DEFAULT 1 (new card types are active by default). Only 4 of 32 are currently active
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same business meaning — whether a card brand is accepted for deposits (1=accepted, 0=rejected) — with the downstream adding only DWH implementation notes about type widening and snapshot lag.

current_desc: Primary key identifying the fee group. 1=Default (standard fees), 2=Exempt (no fees), 3=Discount (reduced fees). Stored on customer records; drives withdrawal fee calculation via Trade.CashoutRange
source_desc: Primary key identifying the fee group. 1=Default, 2=Exempt, 3=Discount. Stored in BackOffice.Customer (FK), Trade.CashoutRange (FK). Mapped from PlayerLevel via Billing.PlayerLevelToCashoutFeeGroup and from GuruStatus via Billing.GuruStatusToCashoutFeeGroup. Auto-updated by Billing.ProcessCashoutFeeGroupUpdate when tier changes
judge_reason: Both describe the same primary key with identical enum values; the downstream version adds parenthetical clarifications of each group's fee semantics and omits upstream mapping/automation details, but the business meaning is equivalent.

current_desc: Human-readable fee group name: 'Default', 'Exempt', 'Discount'. Renamed from production `Name` column. Used in reporting to display fee group
judge_reason: source wiki found but no matching column row

current_desc: Primary key identifying the withdrawal processing mode. 0=Manual, 1=Auto Create, 2=Mass Auto Create, 3=Instant Withdrawal. Stored on withdrawal transaction records (Billing.WithdrawToFunding). Note: ID=0 is a legitimate value (Manual mode), NOT a DWH placeholder
source_desc: Primary key identifying the processing mode. 0=Manual, 1=Auto Create, 2=Mass Auto Create, 3=Instant Withdrawal. TINYINT type (0-255). Stored on Billing.WithdrawToFunding and History.WithdrawToFundingAction. Set at withdrawal creation time
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same business meaning: a primary key for withdrawal processing mode with identical enum values; minor differences in referenced tables and added DWH-placeholder note are non-conflicting details.

current_desc: Human-readable mode name. 'Manual', 'Auto Create', 'Mass Auto Create', 'Instant Withdrawal'. Used in BackOffice withdrawal management screens and payout processing reports
source_desc: Human-readable mode name. Unique constraint prevents duplicates. Note: column named CashoutModeName (not just Name) — differs from most Dictionary tables. Used in BackOffice JOINs as the display label (aliased as CashoutMode or EntryMethod)
judge_reason: Both describe a human-readable mode name used in BackOffice contexts; the downstream version simply enumerates specific values and usage screens, which is additive detail consistent with the source.

current_desc: Processing priority weight. Higher values are processed first: 0=Manual (lowest), 10=Auto Create, 20=Mass Auto Create, 30=Instant Withdrawal (highest). Used by payout processing to determine execution order
source_desc: Processing priority weight — higher values are processed first. 0=Manual (lowest), 10=Auto, 20=Mass Auto, 30=Instant (highest). DEFAULT 100 for new modes (high priority by default). Used by payout processing to determine execution order
judge_reason: Both descriptions convey the same business meaning of a processing priority weight with matching numeric scale and purpose; minor label differences (e.g. 'Auto' vs 'Auto Create') and omission of the DEFAULT 100 detail do not change analyst interpretation.

current_desc: Primary key identifying the withdrawal reason. Range 1-19. Stored in Billing.Withdraw, History.WithdrawAction. Default 16 (user request) in WithdrawRequestAdd. Special processing for IN (12, 14, 15) in WithdrawToFundingProcess. Joined by 15+ BackOffice/Billing/Trade procedures. Values: 1=Adjustment, 2=Partners withdraw, 3=Risk Refund, 4=Negative Balance adjustment, 5=Withdraw fees adjustment, 6=Block account -- Not communicative, 7=3rd party payment, 8=Bonus abuse adjustment, 9=Returned withdraw, 10=Technical issue -- Customer side, 11=Underage, 12=Foreclose account, 13=Test, 14=PI Payment, 15=Affiliate Payment, 16=Requested by User, 17=Failed Verification, 18=Transfered by CryptoWallet, 19=ForClose(GAP)
judge_reason: cannot locate source wiki — manual lookup required

current_desc: Primary key. DWH values: 0=N/A (placeholder), 1=Pending, 2=InProcess, 3=Processed, 4=Canceled. Note: production has 17 states (IDs 5-17 missing from DWH). Stored in withdrawal request records and updated as requests progress
source_desc: Primary key identifying the withdrawal lifecycle state. 1=Pending, 2=InProcess, 3=Processed, 4=Canceled, 5=Partially Processed, 6=Payment Sent, 7=Rejected, 8=RejectedByProvider, 9=PendingByProvider, 10=SentToProvider, 11=SentToBilling, 12=ReceivedByBilling, 13=Failed, 14=Pending Review, 15=Under Review, 16=Reversed, 17=Partially Reversed. current_desc: Human-readable status label. Values: "N/A", "Pending", "InProcess", "Processed", "Canceled". UNIQUE at production level (not enforced in DWH DDL). Used in reports and monitoring

source_desc: Primary key. Values 1–7. Referenced by Billing.Withdraw via FK. Passed as @ClientWithdrawReasonID to WithdrawalService_WithdrawRequestAdd, WithdrawRequestAdd, UpsertWithdraw
